title: 'Authentic storytelling' | Starbucks ramps up employee-generated content with launch of TikTok scheme
tldr: Starbucks is formally turning baristas into content creators through a structured TikTok partnership, framing it as authentic employee storytelling. A clear, branded EGC program with governance worth dissecting.
why_fresh: named-brand EGC program launch with governance angle
angle: How brands can build EGC programs that feel authentic without losing content governance — using Starbucks as the case.

title: Storytelling in the Workplace: Elevating Employee Created Content
tldr: A practical look at turning employee-created content into a strategic storytelling asset inside organizations. Directly relevant to Igor's EGC and human-centered content interests.
why_fresh: EGC framed as workplace storytelling discipline
angle: A framework for elevating employee-created content from random clips to a coherent corporate narrative.

title: Social media marketers are stuck in a burnout trap. Here's how to break free
tldr: A cross-country study of brand social marketers finds a profession in chronic burnout from always-on demands. A human-centered look at who actually makes the content we consume.
why_fresh: new multi-country research on creator/marketer wellbeing
angle: The hidden human cost behind 'always-on' brand content — and what sustainable content ops look like.

title: 3 steps for embracing a community-first strategy
tldr: WWF's comms lead argues brands should stop treating audiences as passive recipients and build genuine community instead. A shift from broadcast to participation.
angle: Why community-first beats campaign-first — and how content strategy changes when you stop leading every conversation.

title: 2026 Digital News Report: Navigating journalism amid a continued crisis of trust
tldr: Reuters Institute's flagship report on how people consume news amid eroding trust and shifting platforms. Essential annual data on media-consumption habits.
angle: What the 2026 Digital News Report reveals about how trust shapes what content people actually consume.

title: One-third of brand value 'created in culture', says M&C
tldr: A new framework claims to empirically quantify how cultural chatter (e.g. Reddit) builds brand value. Useful new lens on share of voice and narrative economics.
why_fresh: new measurement framework for cultural brand value
angle: Can you actually measure brand value created in culture? A look at the new frameworks trying to.

title: Burson Bags Cannes PR Grand Prix For KitKat Heist
tldr: The KitKat 'heist' campaign won the Cannes PR Grand Prix — a concrete, award-winning campaign breakdown worth studying for earned-media mechanics.
angle: Anatomy of the KitKat heist: what an earned-media Grand Prix winner teaches about story scouting.

title: My Job Changed and Nobody Told Me: What AI Visibility Means for Marketers
tldr: Argues AI visibility (showing up in AI answers) has quietly redefined paid, earned, owned, and shared media roles. A pragmatic reframe of where discovery now happens.
why_fresh: AI visibility reframing of PESO roles
angle: AI visibility is the new SEO — how comms roles shift when answer engines become the discovery layer.

title: Advertisers Are Good at Getting Human Attention. Can They Stand Out to A.I.?
tldr: As AI agents mediate discovery and buying, advertisers face a new challenge: optimizing content for machines, not just humans. A genuine shift in who content is 'for'.
angle: When your audience is an algorithm: writing content for AI agents vs. human attention.

title: Cannes Briefing: Creativity is moving beyond the agency model
tldr: For the first time, a growing number of CMOs are sourcing creative beyond traditional agencies — creators, in-house teams, and platforms. A structural shift in content production.
angle: The post-agency creative stack: what happens to content quality when CMOs go beyond agencies.

title: State of the Consumer 2026: When tech acceleration and cost pressures collide
tldr: McKinsey's annual read on shifting consumer behavior at the intersection of AI adoption and cost pressure. Useful raw material for audience-habits storytelling.
angle: What the 2026 consumer mood means for how brands should pace and price their content.

title: 5 Signs Your Financial Content Program Has a Credibility Problem
tldr: AI engines and buyers now reward named, credentialed experts — and Contently flags five symptoms of a content credibility gap, with fixes. Concrete E-E-A-T era guidance.
angle: Why named experts now beat brand bylines in the age of AI-mediated trust.

title: Case Study: Heinz Look Familiar?
tldr: A campaign breakdown of Heinz's 'Look Familiar?' work — concrete creative case study material Igor can mine for storytelling craft.
angle: How Heinz turns brand recognition into participatory storytelling.

title: What 19th-Century Media Can Teach Us About the Future
tldr: Argues media is shaped not just by technology but by infrastructure, law, and society — using early American press history as a lens on today's fragmentation. A smart, idea-rich angle.
why_fresh: fresh historical framing of media-business shifts
angle: What the 1800s press teaches us about today's platform-dependent media economics.

title: Beyond the feed: How to build an agent-ready brand in the AI age
tldr: Guidance on structuring brand content and data so AI agents can discover and represent you. Part of the emerging 'agent-ready brand' discipline.
angle: Structuring your content for the agent web: the new fundamentals of discoverability.
